feat: replace Telegram bot with Nuxt 3 web app (#1)

Replaces the old Netlify-based Telegram bot with a full Nuxt 3 web app
serving as both the **Telegram Mini App** and the **Flutter WebView**
target.

## What changed
- Removed: old `netlify/functions/` webhook/notification bot code and
tests
- Added: full Nuxt 3 app with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, shadcn-vue,
Pinia, Axios

## Features
- **Auth**: login, register, email verification
- **Events**: list, detail, join/leave, participant view
- **Map**: alumni city map with vue-leaflet markers
- **Profile**: view, edit, avatar upload

## Infrastructure
- `Dockerfile`: Node.js server on port 3000
- `docker-stack.yml`: service `mobile` on `iu_alumni_network`,
`NUXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE` env var
- CI/CD: PR → testing deploy, push to main → production deploy (same
`iu_alumni_mobile` Swarm stack)

> **Note**: `iu-alumni-infra` PR #23 must be merged to update nginx
proxy from port 80 → 3000.

commit.message:
Fix invisible dirty indicator and stale git status after save in file editor (#524)

- [x] Fix Bug 1: Replace undefined `bg-ctp-peach`/`text-ctp-peach` with
`bg-ctp-warning`/`text-ctp-warning` in `TabBar.ts`
- [x] Fix Bug 1: Replace `bg-ctp-peach` with `bg-ctp-warning` in
`FileViewer.ts`
- [x] Fix Bug 2: Call `fileState.triggerRefresh()` after successful file
save in `FileViewer.ts`
- [x] Fix e2e test `find-replace.spec.ts`: update `.bg-ctp-peach`
selectors to `.bg-ctp-warning` to match renamed CSS class

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>[LOW] No dirty indicator on tabs; file tree not updated
after save</issue_title>
> <issue_description>## Description
> 
> Two related bugs in the files plugin editor:
> 
> 1. **No dirty file indicator in tab** — When editing a file, the tab
does not show the peach dot indicator for unsaved changes. The dot
element renders but is invisible because the Tailwind class
`bg-ctp-peach` does not exist in the `@theme` block.
> 2. **File tree git status not updated after save** — After saving a
file (Cmd+S), the file tree does not show the file as modified (`M`
badge). The save handler writes to disk and clears dirty state, but
never signals the file tree to re-fetch git status.
> 
> > Possibly a duplicate of / related to Agent-Clubhouse/Clubhouse#510
(undefined Catppuccin color classes).
> 
> ## Root Cause
> 
> ### Bug 1: Invisible dirty dot
> 
> `TabBar.ts` uses `bg-ctp-peach` for the dirty indicator dot, but
`ctp-peach` is **not defined** in `src/renderer/index.css`'s `@theme`
block. The only defined color utilities are the semantic ones
(`ctp-warning`, `ctp-error`, `ctp-info`, `ctp-success`, `ctp-accent`,
plus the base/surface/text palette). Since `bg-ctp-peach` produces no
CSS output, the dot renders as an invisible element.
> 
> **Code pointers:**
> 
> - `src/renderer/plugins/builtin/files/TabBar.ts:174` — pinned tab
dirty dot uses `bg-ctp-peach`
> - `src/renderer/plugins/builtin/files/TabBar.ts:224` — regular tab
dirty dot uses `bg-ctp-peach`
> - `src/renderer/plugins/builtin/files/TabBar.ts:216` — close button
uses `text-ctp-peach` (also undefined)
> - `src/renderer/plugins/builtin/files/FileViewer.ts:385` — file header
dirty dot likely uses same undefined class
> - `src/renderer/index.css:6-19` — `@theme` block only defines semantic
colors, not raw Catppuccin names
> 
> ### Bug 2: File tree git status not refreshed after save
> 
> When a file is saved via `handleSave` or `saveFile`, the handler
writes the file via IPC and clears the tab's dirty flag, but **never
calls `fileState.triggerRefresh()`**. The file tree relies on a
file-watcher subscription with a 500ms debounce to eventually refresh
git status, but this is unreliable because:
> - The app's own IPC write may not trigger the watcher consistently
> - Even when it does, the 500ms debounce introduces a noticeable lag
> - No explicit signal connects save completion → tree git status reload
> 
> **Code pointers:**
> 
> - `src/renderer/plugins/builtin/files/FileViewer.ts:251-264` —
`saveFile()`: writes file, clears dirty, but no `triggerRefresh()`
> - `src/renderer/plugins/builtin/files/FileViewer.ts:275-289` —
`handleSave()`: same pattern, no `triggerRefresh()`
> - `src/renderer/plugins/builtin/files/state.ts:404-407` —
`triggerRefresh()` exists but is never called from save paths
> - `src/renderer/plugins/builtin/files/FileTree.ts:480-495` —
subscribes to `refreshCount` to reload git status
> - `src/renderer/plugins/builtin/files/FileTree.ts:509-522` — file
watcher debounce (500ms) is the only current trigger
> 
> ## Suggested Fix
> 
> ### Bug 1
> Replace `bg-ctp-peach` / `text-ctp-peach` with a defined semantic
color. `bg-ctp-warning` (yellow/gold) is the closest semantic match for
"unsaved changes":
> 
> ```typescript
> // TabBar.ts line 174 (pinned tab dirty dot)
> - className: 'absolute top-1 right-1 w-1.5 h-1.5 rounded-full
bg-ctp-peach',
> + className: 'absolute top-1 right-1 w-1.5 h-1.5 rounded-full
bg-ctp-warning',
> 
> // TabBar.ts line 224 (regular tab dirty dot)
> - className: 'w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-ctp-peach',
> + className: 'w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-ctp-warning',
> 
> // TabBar.ts line 216 (close button text when dirty)
> - 'text-ctp-peach hover:bg-ctp-surface1'
> + 'text-ctp-warning hover:bg-ctp-surface1'
> ```
> 
> ### Bug 2
> Call `triggerRefresh()` after a successful file save to immediately
update the tree:
> 
> ```typescript
> // FileViewer.ts saveFile() — after line 260
> fileState.triggerRefresh();
> 
> // FileViewer.ts handleSave() — after line 280
> fileState.triggerRefresh();
> ```
> 
> ## Files
> 
> | File | Lines | Issue |
> |------|-------|-------|
> | `src/renderer/plugins/builtin/files/TabBar.ts` | 174, 216, 224 |
Uses undefined `bg-ctp-peach` / `text-ctp-peach` |
> | `src/renderer/plugins/builtin/files/FileViewer.ts` | 251-264,
275-289, 385 | Missing `triggerRefresh()` after save; undefined color
class |
> | `src/renderer/plugins/builtin/files/state.ts` | 404-407 |
`triggerRefresh()` exists but unused from save paths |
> | `src/renderer/plugins/builtin/files/FileTree.ts` | 480-495, 509-522
| Only refreshes on watcher/manual, not on save signal |
> | `src/renderer/index.css` | 6-19 | `@theme` missing `ctp-peach`
definition |</issue_description>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> <comments>
> </comments>
> 


</details>

Implemented IdentifierNode refactoring from IdentifierResolutionPlan.md #857 (#861)

* Fix C++20 point-of-declaration: sizeof(x) in own initializer

Per [basic.scope.pdecl], a variable name is visible from the point
immediately after its declarator, before the initializer. This means
sizeof(x) in 'int x = sizeof(x)' is well-formed and evaluates to 4.

Root cause: three-part failure chain:
1. Parser pre-insertion missing: x was not in gSymbolTable when the
   initializer was parsed, so sizeof(x) was mis-parsed as sizeof of an
   unknown UserDefined type with size 0.
2. sizeof disambiguation: the UserDefined/size-0 check did not look up
   the symbol table to detect known variables.
3. Codegen pre-registration missing: even after fixing the parser to
   produce a from_expression sizeof node, the codegen's local symbol_table
   did not contain x when generateSizeofIr evaluated the initializer.

Changes:
- src/SymbolTable.h: add replace_variable() for updating a pre-inserted
  stub with its final node.
- src/Parser_Statements.cpp: in parse_variable_declaration(), pre-insert
  a stub VariableDeclarationNode (no init) for block-scope variables
  before parsing the initializer; replace with the real node after.
- src/Parser_Expr_PrimaryUnary.cpp: sizeof disambiguation now falls
  through to expression parsing when the identifier resolves to a
  VariableDeclarationNode in the symbol table.
- src/CodeGen_Stmt_Decl.cpp: register the variable in the codegen's
  local symbol_table before evaluating the initializer expression so
  that lookupDeclaration() succeeds during sizeof evaluation.
- tests/test_initializer_point_of_declaration_ret4.cpp: regression test.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add NON_STANDARD_BEHAVIOR.md cataloguing 33 C++20 deviations

Documents all known places where FlashCpp deviates from the ISO C++20
standard, grouped into 9 categories:
- Lookup/overload: ADL missing, free-function ops invisible, first-overload
  fallback, mangling wrong-type fallbacks
- Lambda: capture-all by odr-use, constexpr capture limits
- Templates: NTTP deduction, silent instantiation fallback
- Constexpr: constructor body ignored, consteval unenforced
- ABI: SysV struct passing, pointer_depth=0 in 7+ sites
- Exception handling: __cpp_exceptions absent, cross-refs EXCEPTION_HANDLING.md
- Preprocessor: _Pragma partial, __GNUC__+_MSC_VER both defined, macro lies
- Codegen: synthesised ops return false, new[] skips elements, InternalError

Cross-references existing docs for the 18 already-documented items.

Port of FlashCpp2 commit dbe67b66. When the divisor expression
evaluates into RAX (e.g. a function return value), the subsequent
mov RAX, <dividend> overwrites it before IDIV, producing wrong
results (effectively RAX/RAX = 1).

Add three helpers to IRConverter_Conv_Arithmetic.h:
- reserveDivisionFixedRegisters(): extracted flush+lock of RAX/RDX
- allocateDivisionScratchRegister(): finds a scratch reg from
  RCX/R8-R11 avoiding specified registers
- preserveDivisorAcrossRaxMove(): if divisor_reg==RAX, copies it
  to a scratch register before the dividend-to-RAX move

Apply to handleDivide, handleUnsignedDivide, handleModulo,
handleDivAssign (restructured to check FP type before setup),
and handleModAssign. Also add missing regAlloc.release(RDX)
in handleModAssign.

Test: test_divide_divisor_in_rax_ret5 (25 / get_divisor() = 5)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix float conversion XMM reuse under register pressure

Port of FlashCpp2 commit 423edce9. In handleFloatToFloat, source_xmm
and result_xmm are allocated sequentially. Under XMM register pressure
the allocator may return the same register for both, causing
regAlloc.release(source_xmm) to free the result register before it
is stored.

Guard the release: only release source_xmm when it differs from
result_xmm.

Test: test_float_to_double_xmm_pressure_ret120 exercises float-to-double
conversion with 15 live float values to maximise XMM pressure.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: only set is_global_or_static when global symbol lookup actually succeeds

The `is_global_or_static = true` was placed outside the `if (sym.has_value())`
block in analyzeAddressExpression, causing it to be set even when the global
symbol table lookup returned nullopt. This resulted in returning
AddressComponents with final_type=Void and final_size_bits=0, which could
cause incorrect memory accesses downstream in ComputeAddress generation.

The old detectGlobalOrStaticVar only set this flag when the lookup succeeded
AND the symbol had valid type information. This restores that safety check.

* Use optional<TemplateDepthGuard> instead of ScopedState + manual ++ for template depth

Replace the mixed ScopedState/manual-increment pattern with the idiomatic
conditional TemplateDepthGuard used everywhere else. Fixes indentation and
improves consistency. No behavioral change.

* Revert simple assignment StaticMember change: isValid() assignment is fine here since gsi has no type/size fields

* Enforce C++20 [temp.res]/9 Phase 1 two-phase name lookup

Non-dependent names used inside a template body must be visible at
template definition time. Using a name declared after the template
definition is now rejected as ill-formed.

Implementation:
- Parser.h: Added phase1_cutoff_line_, phase1_cutoff_file_idx_,
  phase1_violation_token_ fields. createBoundIdentifier() now calls
  checkPhase1 for global-scope FunctionDeclarationNode,
  TemplateFunctionDeclarationNode, DeclarationNode, and
  VariableDeclarationNode.
- Parser_Templates_Inst_Deduction.cpp: reparse_template_function_body()
  sets the cutoff from the saved template body opening-brace token and
  throws CompileError after parse if a violation was detected.
- Parser_Templates_Substitution.cpp: Reverted to promoting
  Unresolved -> concrete (the reparse path handles the real check;
  throwing here caused false positives for 'this', args_0, etc.).
- Parser_Templates_Lazy.cpp: Re-throw CompileError before the two
  catch(std::exception&) blocks that were swallowing it.
- main.cpp: Catch CompileError in the parse phase and exit 1.

Tests:
- Added test_template_nondependent_not_visible_fail.cpp
- Added test_template_rebind_late_global_fail.cpp
- Removed test_template_rebind_late_global_ret0.cpp (was testing
  the old non-standard MSVC-like behaviour)

All 1361 tests pass; 28 _fail tests fail as expected.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Port constexpr static member binding fixes from FlashCpp2

Honor parser-bound static member identifiers in constexpr evaluation
before falling back to unqualified lookup. Fixes four cases:
- Simple static constexpr member (Box::value vs global value)
- Qualified function targets in constexpr calls
- Static member objects (inner.value uses Outer::inner)
- Nested static member objects

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix createBoundIdentifier misclassifying enum-typed variables as EnumConstant

DeclarationNode with Type::Enum can be either an enumerator constant (e.g., Red)
or a variable/parameter of enum type (e.g., `Color c`). The old code classified
both as EnumConstant. Now we verify via EnumTypeInfo::findEnumerator that the
identifier is actually an enumerator value before setting EnumConstant binding.
Variables/parameters of enum type correctly fall through to Local/Global.

* Port identifier binding parity fixes

Add the missing constexpr identifier-binding regression tests and backport the parser, constexpr, template, and static-initializer fixes needed to make FlashCpp's identifier-binding suite pass after auditing newer FlashCpp2 work.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf(db): add 14 missing PostgreSQL indexes

Tables impactées et requêtes couvertes :
- phase_outcomes(pattern_id) — AVG(duration_s) WHERE pattern_id=? à chaque phase (engine.py)
- phase_outcomes(created_at) — purge / fenêtres temporelles
- epic_runs(created_at DESC) — cockpit 24h/7d windows (multiple queries)
- epic_runs(session_id) — UPDATE WHERE session_id=? à chaque phase (engine.py)
- epic_runs(status, created_at DESC) — cockpit failure counts par fenêtre
- team_fitness_history(technology, phase_type) — boards WHERE technology=?
  (idx_tfh_key commence par agent_id → inutilisable pour filtre tech seul)
- team_fitness_history(snapshot_date DESC) — évolution temporelle
- team_okr(technology, kpi_name) — UPDATE KPI current par contexte
- team_okr(team_key) — ORDER BY team_key, kpi_name
- agent_assignments(project_id) — WHERE project_id=? (seul PK = agent_id)
- agent_scores(agent_id, epic_id) — composite selection.py WHERE agent_id+epic_id
- messages(from_agent, timestamp DESC) — cockpit activity feed filtrée
- llm_traces(session_id, created_at DESC) — trace lookup par session
- tool_calls(agent_id, timestamp DESC) — fenêtres d'activité agent
+ rl_experience(created_at DESC) + admin_audit_log(resource_type, resource_id)

Indexes ajoutés dans migrations.py (existing DBs) ET schema_pg.sql (fresh installs).
Tous idempotents (CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
